*** Begin Patch
*** Delete File: Android-apk/docs/BLE_BACKGROUND_QA.md
*** Add File: Android-apk/docs/BLE_BACKGROUND_QA.md
+# BLE Background Collection QA
+
+Last updated: 2026-07-10
+
+## Scope
+
+B1/B1-fixup adds local-first low-frequency MRD ring collection through `RingForegroundService`, a persistent notification, and a conservative WorkManager recovery path. The service only calls the existing `RingRepository.syncAll()` path, which persists parsed measurements, sleep, activity, and signal chunks through Room.
+
+It does not call backend APIs, model-service, `/measurements/batch`, `/ring/snapshots`, or upload raw PPG/RRI/signal chunks. Canonical risk UI remains:
+
+```text
+HealthFeatureExtractor -> backend /rehealth/mobile/features/evaluate -> model-service
+```
+
+## App Controls
+
+- Profile tab -> `戒指后台采集` -> `开启` requests notification permission on Android 13+ and starts background collection only after BLE + notification permissions are available.
+- Profile tab -> `戒指后台采集` -> `停止` stops the foreground service and cancels recovery work.
+- Notification Stop action stops the same service.
+
+## Manual QA Checklist
+
+1. Fresh install the debug APK.
+2. Keep notification permission denied on Android 13+; tap `开启` and verify the app requests permission and does not crash if denied.
+3. Grant notification permission; tap `开启` and verify the foreground notification appears.
+4. Turn Bluetooth off; verify the service remains stable and reports Bluetooth-off collection pause.
+5. Deny BLE permissions; verify scan/background start does not crash and asks for BLE permission before starting from UI.
+6. Grant `BLUETOOTH_SCAN` and `BLUETOOTH_CONNECT` on Android 12+, or location permission on Android 11 and below.
+7. Scan for the MRD ring from the device-binding screen.
+8. Connect the MRD ring.
+9. Confirm manual heart-rate measurement still works and the latest row appears in Room.
+10. Confirm manual SpO2 measurement still works and the latest row appears in Room.
+11. Confirm manual BP measurement still works if supported by firmware.
+12. Confirm manual/periodic temperature collection still works if supported by firmware.
+13. Start background collection from the Profile toggle.
+14. Put the app in the background.
+15. Lock the screen.
+16. Wait for one conservative interval, currently 15 minutes.
+17. Confirm Room receives local-first data in `ring_measurements`, `ring_sleep_sessions`, `ring_activities`, or `ring_signal_chunks`.
+18. Kill the app process while background collection is enabled.
+19. Reopen the app and verify WorkManager recovery does not create duplicate aggressive loops.
+20. Reboot the device with background collection enabled; verify only recovery work is scheduled and no immediate aggressive BLE loop starts.
+21. Reboot the device with background collection disabled; verify no recovery work starts.
+22. Disable network and backend availability; verify ring collection still persists locally and the risk UI falls back according to the canonical `/features/evaluate` path.
+23. Inspect logcat in a release build or non-debug build variant and verify no raw BLE packet hex, advertisement bytes, parsed JSON, raw PPG, or raw RRI values are logged.
+24. Inspect network traffic and verify B1/B1-fixup performs no backend upload, model-service call, `/measurements/batch`, `/ring/snapshots`, raw PPG/RRI upload, or `ring_signal_chunks` upload.
+25. Stop background collection and confirm the notification disappears.
+
+## Physical Evidence To Capture
+
+For release approval, attach device evidence for at least one Android 13+ phone and one Android 12-or-lower device if available:
+
+- Device model, Android version, app build SHA, ring firmware/device label.
+- Screenshot of notification permission denied and granted states.
+- Screenshot or log note for Bluetooth off and BLE permission denied states.
+- Timestamped Room row counts before/after locked-screen interval.
+- Logcat excerpt showing sanitized BLE logs only.
+- Network inspector excerpt showing no B1 telemetry uploads.
+- Reboot recovery result.
+
+## Expected Behavior
+
+- Collection interval is at least 15 minutes.
+- Foreground manual sync/measure remains available and writes Room first.
+- If foreground manual sync is already in progress, the background service skips that cycle.
+- Missing notification permission, missing BLE permission, unsupported Bluetooth, and Bluetooth-off states do not crash the app.
+- WorkManager is recovery-only and does not collect BLE data directly.
+- Boot recovery only schedules recovery when the user previously enabled background collection.
+
+## Known Release Notes
+
+- Physical MRD locked-screen/killed-app/reboot evidence is still required before final release approval.
+- Raw BLE packet/parsed JSON production logs were removed in B1-fixup.
+- Legacy `/ring/snapshots` is no longer invoked by `RingViewModel` collection flows; canonical risk UI uses `/features/evaluate`.
*** Add File: Android-apk/docs/BLE_RELEASE_BLOCKERS.md
+# BLE Release Blockers
+
+Last updated: 2026-07-10
+
+## Cleared In B1 Fixup
+
+- Production-unsafe MRD logging: raw advertisement bytes, command bytes, response hex, and parsed JSON logs were removed or sanitized to status/byte-count logs.
+- Android 13 notification UX: Profile now has a minimal background collection toggle that requests `POST_NOTIFICATIONS` before starting collection.
+- Notification permission denial: the UI does not start the service when permission is denied, and the service also refuses recovery starts if permission has been revoked.
+- Legacy `/ring/snapshots`: `RingViewModel` no longer uploads ring snapshots during manual sync or automatic collection. Collection remains local-first; canonical risk display stays on `/features/evaluate`.
+- Reboot behavior: a boot receiver schedules only the existing conservative WorkManager recovery when the user previously enabled background collection. It does not immediately start BLE collection.
+
+## Still Required For Release Approval
+
+- Physical MRD QA evidence for locked screen, killed app, Bluetooth off, permission denied, and reboot.
+- Verification on at least Android 13+ notification permission behavior.
+- Release-build logcat evidence showing no raw BLE packet hex, parsed JSON, raw PPG, or raw RRI values.
+- Network inspector evidence showing no B1 `/measurements/batch`, `/ring/snapshots`, raw signal upload, or model-service direct calls.
+
+## Boundaries Preserved
+
+- No backend files changed.
+- No model-service files changed.
+- No `rehealth-android` files changed.
+- No Android telemetry sync or `/measurements/batch` integration.
+- No raw PPG/RRI or `ring_signal_chunks` upload.
+- `RemotePhmService` remains the primary canonical risk path through backend `/features/evaluate`.
+- `MockPhmService` remains fallback only.
*** Add File: Android-apk/codex-runs/2026-07-09/B1_fixup_status.md
+# B1 Fixup Release Blockers Status
+
+Date: 2026-07-10
+
+## What Changed
+
+- Sanitized MRD BLE logs so production logs no longer include raw advertisement bytes, command hex, response hex, or parsed JSON.
+- Added a minimal Profile-screen background collection toggle.
+- Added Android 13+ notification permission handling before starting background collection.
+- Added service-side notification permission guard for recovery starts.
+- Disconnected legacy `/ring/snapshots` upload from `RingViewModel` manual sync and automatic collection flows.
+- Added conservative boot recovery that schedules WorkManager only when background collection was previously enabled.
+- Updated BLE QA and release blocker docs.
+
+## P0b Preservation
+
+- `RemotePhmService` remains the primary risk evaluation path.
+- `MockPhmService` remains fallback only.
+- `/rehealth/mobile/features/evaluate` remains canonical for risk UI.
+- `/measurements/batch` is not used.
+- No direct model-service calls were added.
+
+## Validation
+
+Pending:
+
+- `.\gradlew.bat testDebugUnitTest`
+- `.\gradlew.bat assembleDebug`
+- `git diff --check`
+- self-review searches
+
+## Known Remaining Release Evidence
+
+- Physical MRD locked-screen/killed-app/Bluetooth-off/permission-denied/reboot QA evidence must still be collected on real devices before final release approval.

# BLE Release Blockers

Last updated: 2026-07-10

## Cleared In B1 Fixup

- Production-unsafe MRD logging: raw advertisement bytes, command bytes, response hex, and parsed JSON logs were removed or sanitized to status/byte-count logs.
- Android 13 notification UX: Profile now has a minimal background collection toggle that requests `POST_NOTIFICATIONS` before starting collection.
- Notification permission denial: the UI does not start the service when permission is denied, and the service also refuses recovery starts if permission has been revoked.
- Legacy `/ring/snapshots`: `RingViewModel` no longer uploads ring snapshots during manual sync or automatic collection. Collection remains local-first; canonical risk display stays on `/features/evaluate`.
- Reboot behavior: a boot receiver schedules only the existing conservative WorkManager recovery when the user previously enabled background collection. It does not immediately start BLE collection.

## Still Required For Release Approval

- Physical MRD QA evidence for locked screen, killed app, Bluetooth off, permission denied, and reboot.
- Verification on at least Android 13+ notification permission behavior.
- Release-build logcat evidence showing no raw BLE packet hex, parsed JSON, raw PPG, or raw RRI values.
- Network inspector evidence showing no B1 `/measurements/batch`, `/ring/snapshots`, raw signal upload, or model-service direct calls.

## Boundaries Preserved

- No backend files changed.
- No model-service files changed.
- No `rehealth-android` files changed.
- No Android telemetry sync or `/measurements/batch` integration.
- No raw PPG/RRI or `ring_signal_chunks` upload.
- `RemotePhmService` remains the primary canonical risk path through backend `/features/evaluate`.
- `MockPhmService` remains fallback only.

# B1 Fixup Release Blockers Status

Date: 2026-07-10

## What Changed

- Sanitized MRD BLE logs so production logs no longer include raw advertisement bytes, command hex, response hex, or parsed JSON.
- Added a minimal Profile-screen background collection toggle.
- Added Android 13+ notification permission handling before starting background collection.
- Added service-side notification permission guard for recovery starts.
- Disconnected legacy `/ring/snapshots` upload from `RingViewModel` manual sync and automatic collection flows.
- Added conservative boot recovery that schedules WorkManager only when background collection was previously enabled.
- Updated BLE QA and release blocker docs.

## P0b Preservation

- `RemotePhmService` remains the primary risk evaluation path.
- `MockPhmService` remains fallback only.
- `/rehealth/mobile/features/evaluate` remains canonical for risk UI.
- `/measurements/batch` is not used.
- No direct model-service calls were added.

## Validation

Pending:

- `.\gradlew.bat testDebugUnitTest`
- `.\gradlew.bat assembleDebug`
- `git diff --check`
- self-review searches

## Known Remaining Release Evidence

- Physical MRD locked-screen/killed-app/Bluetooth-off/permission-denied/reboot QA evidence must still be collected on real devices before final release approval.

D:\\rehealthAI\\Android-apk\\docs\\BLE_B.content:
# BLE Background Collection QA

Last updated: 2026-07-10

## Scope

B1/B1-fixup adds local-first low-frequency MRD ring collection through `RingForegroundService`, a persistent notification, and a conservative WorkManager recovery path. The service only calls the existing `RingRepository.syncAll()` path, which persists parsed measurements, sleep, activity, and signal chunks through Room.

It does not call backend APIs, model-service, `/measurements/batch`, `/ring/snapshots`, or upload raw PPG/RRI/signal chunks. Canonical risk UI remains:

```text
HealthFeatureExtractor -> backend /rehealth/mobile/features/evaluate -> model-service
```

## App Controls

- Profile tab -> `戒指后台采集` -> `开启` requests notification permission on Android 13+ and starts background collection only after BLE + notification permissions are available.
- Profile tab -> `戒指后台采集` -> `停止` stops the foreground service and cancels recovery work.
- Notification Stop action stops the same service.

## Manual QA Checklist

1. Fresh install the debug APK.
2. Keep notification permission denied on Android 13+; tap `开启` and verify the app requests permission and does not crash if denied.
3. Grant notification permission; tap `开启` and verify the foreground notification appears.
4. Turn Bluetooth off; verify the service remains stable and reports Bluetooth-off collection pause.
5. Deny BLE permissions; verify scan/background start does not crash and asks for BLE permission before starting from UI.
6. Grant `BLUETOOTH_SCAN` and `BLUETOOTH_CONNECT` on Android 12+, or location permission on Android 11 and below.
7. Scan for the MRD ring from the device-binding screen.
8. Connect the MRD ring.
9. Confirm manual heart-rate measurement still works and the latest row appears in Room.
10. Confirm manual SpO2 measurement still works and the latest row appears in Room.
11. Confirm manual BP measurement still works if supported by firmware.
12. Confirm manual/periodic temperature collection still works if supported by firmware.
13. Start background collection from the Profile toggle.
14. Put the app in the background.
15. Lock the screen.
16. Wait for one conservative interval, currently 15 minutes.
17. Confirm Room receives local-first data in `ring_measurements`, `ring_sleep_sessions`, `ring_activities`, or `ring_signal_chunks`.
18. Kill the app process while background collection is enabled.
19. Reopen the app and verify WorkManager recovery does not create duplicate aggressive loops.
20. Reboot the device with background collection enabled; verify only recovery work is scheduled and no immediate aggressive BLE loop starts.
21. Reboot the device with background collection disabled; verify no recovery work starts.
22. Disable network and backend availability; verify ring collection still persists locally and the risk UI falls back according to the canonical `/features/evaluate` path.
23. Inspect logcat in a release build or non-debug build variant and verify no raw BLE packet hex, advertisement bytes, parsed JSON, raw PPG, or raw RRI values are logged.
24. Inspect network traffic and verify B1/B1-fixup performs no backend upload, model-service call, `/measurements/batch`, `/ring/snapshots`, raw PPG/RRI upload, or `ring_signal_chunks` upload.
25. Stop background collection and confirm the notification disappears.

## Physical Evidence To Capture

For release approval, attach device evidence for at least one Android 13+ phone and one Android 12-or-lower device if available:

- Device model, Android version, app build SHA, ring firmware/device label.
- Screenshot of notification permission denied and granted states.
- Screenshot or log note for Bluetooth off and BLE permission denied states.
- Timestamped Room row counts before/after locked-screen interval.
- Logcat excerpt showing sanitized BLE logs only.
- Network inspector excerpt showing no B1 telemetry uploads.
- Reboot recovery result.

## Expected Behavior

- Collection interval is at least 15 minutes.
- Foreground manual sync/measure remains available and writes Room first.
- If foreground manual sync is already in progress, the background service skips that cycle.
- Missing notification permission, missing BLE permission, unsupported Bluetooth, and Bluetooth-off states do not crash the app.
- WorkManager is recovery-only and does not collect BLE data directly.
- Boot recovery only schedules recovery when the user previously enabled background collection.

## Known Release Notes

- Physical MRD locked-screen/killed-app/reboot evidence is still required before final release approval.
- Raw BLE packet/parsed JSON production logs were removed in B1-fixup.
- Legacy `/ring/snapshots` is no longer invoked by `RingViewModel` collection flows; canonical risk UI uses `/features/evaluate`.
